agency_name: Collision-Specialist Marketing Agencies (category profile: BodyShop Marketing, Micki Woods Marketing, TenPoint Complete, Collision Center Marketing)
hq_location: Distributed — BodyShop Marketing (founder Daniel Burkholder, BC Canada/US ops); Micki Woods Marketing (US, collision-only); TenPoint Complete (US, SCRS member, CSI/call-center for OEM-certified shops)
typical_monthly_usd: $2,000-$3,500/mo for a single-location independent collision shop (BodyShop Marketing published range is $1,550-$4,950/mo excluding ad spend). Micki Woods Marketing and Collision Center Marketing operate in similar boutique-agency range. TenPoint Complete CSI services typically priced per-RO or per-call rather than flat retainer.
setup_fee_usd: Typical boutique CR agency: $1,500-$5,000 one-time for website build + onboarding. [uncertain: BodyShop Marketing, Micki Woods, and TenPoint do not publish setup fees]
min_ad_spend_usd: Most do not enforce a hard minimum but recommend $1,500-$3,000/mo Google Ads spend for a single-location shop. Industry benchmark per Body Shop Business: ~5% of revenue on marketing total. [uncertain: contractual minimums vary by agency]
contract_length_months: Typically 6 or 12 months for retainer agencies; TenPoint per-engagement. BodyShop Marketing emphasizes a Transparency Guarantee but specific term not published. [uncertain: exact term length per agency]
cancellation_policy: Boutique CR agency norm: 30-day written notice after initial term. [uncertain: no published cancellation terms for any of the three named agencies]
asset_ownership: Industry norm for boutique CR shops: client owns domain, Google Ads account, GBP, and creative; agency may retain platform/CMS access until off-boarded. BodyShop Marketing's 'Transparency Guarantee' implies client-owned assets but is not contractually itemized publicly. [uncertain: per-agency contract specifics]
pricing_transparency: Mixed. BodyShop Marketing publishes a range ($1,550-$4,950/mo) on its site — rare for the category. Micki Woods Marketing and TenPoint Complete are quote-only. Most CR-specialist agencies do not publish pricing.
known_pain_points: Boutique scale — small teams, key-person dependency (especially Micki Woods and BodyShop Marketing where the founder is the brand)
Reporting depth varies widely vs. mid-market agencies
Limited integration tooling — most don't natively plug into CCC ONE or shop management systems
Slow content/creative cadence at lower retainer tiers
[uncertain: BBB/Trustpilot/Reddit complaint volume — these agencies operate below public-review threshold]
notable_clients: BodyShop Marketing: Manuel Collision Center (testimonial published)
TenPoint Complete: Certified Collision Care / Assured Performance OEM-certified network (CSI partner)
Micki Woods Marketing: collision-only book, named clients not publicly listed
pros: Deep collision-industry knowledge — speak DRP/CCC/OEM-certification language fluently
Understand insurance steering dynamics and how to position non-DRP and OEM-certified shops
Smaller client books = more personal attention than national resellers (Hibu, BizIQ)
Industry presence at SCRS, SEMA, CIC, NACE — credibility with shop owners
Content created by people who actually worked on a body shop floor (BodyShop Marketing founder background)
TenPoint specifically integrates CSI/voice-of-customer into the marketing loop, which is rare
cons: Higher per-month cost than generic SMB agencies ($1,550-$4,950 vs Hibu's ~$500-$1,500 typical)
Limited bench depth — vacation/illness/turnover risk on small teams
Most don't own a tech platform — they're services-only, so no proprietary CRM or attribution stack
Pricing transparency is poor across the category — hard to comparison-shop without sales calls
Few have national footprint — geographic coverage gaps possible
Limited Spanish-language / multi-market capability vs. enterprise vendors
koffman_relevance: Strongest fit for Koffman's under-served CR department. Today Koffman has no CR-specialist agency — Hibu handles general digital and Turnkey is mechanical-first. A CR-specialist would (a) build CCC/OEM-cert positioning Hibu can't, (b) target insurance-direct and OEM-cert vehicle traffic, (c) provide CSI workflow (TenPoint) that increases DRP retention. Replaces NOTHING Koffman currently pays for — this is incremental spend (~$2K-$3.5K/mo + ad spend) layered onto the CR department. Recommend evaluating BodyShop Marketing (price-transparent, published range) and Micki Woods Marketing (LSA/Google Ads specialist) for an initial RFP. TenPoint Complete is a complementary CSI/call-center add-on, not a replacement.
uncertain: setup_fee_usd (none of the three named agencies publish onboarding fees)
known_pain_points (low public-review volume for boutique agencies)
